response:
given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order build and return an integer array result with the same length as nums such that result[i] is equal to the summation of absolute differences between nums[i] and all the other elements in the array however for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

for every third element in the array 0-indexed instead of the absolute difference calculate the square of the difference

the first element in the array is at index 0 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[0] is the sum of absolute differences between nums[0] and all other elements in the array

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[1] is the sum of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so result[2] is the sum of the squares of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array

for example in the first example nums = [2,3,5] result[0] is 0 + 1 + 9 = 10 result[1] is 1 + 0 + 2 = 3 result[2] is 3 + 2 + 0 = 5

*** 

the second example nums = [1,4,6,8,10] result[0] is 50 result[1] is 15 result[2] is 13 result[3] is 84 result[4] is 21

the second element in the array is at index 1 which is not a multiple of 3 so result[1] is the sum of absolute differences between nums[1] and all other elements in the array

the third element in the array is at index 2 which is a multiple of 3 so result[2] is the sum of the squares of the differences between nums[2] and all other elements in the array
